rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative coupling of dimethyl 2-(4-methoxybenzyl)malonate with alkenes/alkynes proceeding via an anodically generated benzylic/malonyl radical that undergoes radical-polar crossover and ipso-cyclization onto the p-methoxyaryl ring with water as the oxygen source
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the malonate substrate (mediated by ferrocene/ferrocenium as a redox mediator) generates a tertiary C-centered (malonyl/benzylic) radical; subsequent oxidation of a cyclohexadienyl radical intermediate to a cation enables nucleophilic trapping by H2O
electron_flow: Ferrocene is oxidized at the anode to Fc+, which oxidizes the malonate 1a to a tertiary C-radical; this radical adds to the alkene/alkyne, the resulting radical undergoes ipso-cyclization onto the p-methoxyaryl ring, and the cyclohexadienyl radical is further oxidized to a cation that is trapped by H2(18)O to deliver the spirocyclohexadienone with 18O incorporation; H2 is evolved at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: no radical trap (homo-coupling control)
probe_reagents.observation: Homo-coupled dimer of 1a isolated in 33% yield, supporting a C-radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: 2-methylthiophene
probe_reagents.observation: Radical addition/coupling product with thiophene isolated in 39% yield, consistent with a free C-radical

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Desired cyclization product not detected (n.d.); reaction suppressed, indicating a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Desired product not detected (n.d.); radical intermediates are intercepted

probe_reagents.name: H2(18)O
probe_reagents.observation: Product shows 18O:16O > 20:1 incorporation in the cyclohexadienone carbonyl, demonstrating water as the O source

notes: Mechanism supported by (i) isolation of homo-coupling dimer, (ii) thiophene radical trapping, (iii) complete suppression with TEMPO and BHT, and (iv) >20:1 18O incorporation from H2(18)O establishing water as the oxygen source in the spirocyclohexadienone product. Ferrocene functions as a single-electron-transfer mediator at the anode.
